blocks.text: DTC P2252: LAF sensor AFS - low terminal voltage

The LAF sensor is mounted on the catalytic converter directly below and detects the oxygen concentration in the exhaust gas and outputs the voltage corresponding to the concentration to the PGM-FI ECU. The LAF sensor has an integrated sensor element and heater, which is controlled by the PGM-FI ECU. The heater is provided to accelerate the activation time of the LAF sensor by heating the sensor element through current control from the PGM-FI ECU to enable stable detection at all times. When voltage is applied to the sensor, oxygen molecules in the exhaust gas move inside the sensor, and the chemical reaction of the oxygen molecules generates an electric current. This current becomes the output of the LAF sensor and is input to the PGM-FI ECU. The PGM-FI ECU compares the target air-fuel ratio with the air-fuel ratio detected by the LAF sensor to control the fuel injection amount. If the AFV terminal voltage of the LAF sensor remains below a predetermined value for a predetermined period of time, the PGM-FI ECU determines a failure and stores the DTC.
Detection frequency, detection sequence, detection time required, detection method type, OBD status
AFC terminal voltage remains below 0.40V for more than 4 seconds.

items.text: Disconnection of code (AFV line) between PGM-FI ECU and LAF sensor

blocks.text: Start the engine, run the radiator fan twice, and then warm up the engine for at least 2 minutes at 3,000 rpm with no load.

If the vehicle is determined to have a fault, a temporary DTC is stored in the PGM-FI ECU memory. If the same failure is detected at the next D/C (two consecutive detections), the PGM-FI warning light comes on and the DTC and freeze data are stored.

If the vehicle is diagnosed and determined to be normal after running consecutive 3D/C runs, the PGM-FI warning light will turn off. In addition, the PGM-FI warning light, temporary DTC, DTC and freeze data are cleared by using the clear command of the scan tool (including HDS) or by removing the 12V battery terminal.
